theme.title: Mixed / Stock-Picker's Market
theme.summary: Technology (+1.72%) and Materials (+0.51%) led the market today, while Health Care (-1.76%) and Consumer Staples (-1.15%) brought up the rear. Growth sectors outpaced the rest, averaging +0.56% versus -0.96% for defensives and +0.03% for value, consistent with risk appetite favoring higher-beta names. That shows up at the index level too: the tech-heavy Nasdaq gained +0.94% while the blue-chip Dow lagged at -0.15%. Small caps moved roughly in line with the broader market, with the Russell 2000 at +0.46%. The VIX fell 2.57% to 16.71, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold added 1.10% to $4,066.70, crude oil was little changed at $79.31, and bitcoin gained 4.04% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
